title: End of Semester Examinations Schedule
body: The end of semester examinations will begin on June 2, 2026. All students are reminded to review the timetable posted on the notice board. Students must arrive 15 minutes early on examination days. No mobile phones allowed in the examination hall.

title: Lecturer-Student Conference
body: We are pleased to announce the annual Lecturer-Student Conference scheduled for May 25, 2026. All students are encouraged to attend. Appointment slots can be booked through the administration office.

title: University Sports Day
body: The annual Sports Day will be held on May 30, 2026 at the university grounds. All students are expected to participate. Events include track and field, football, volleyball, and basketball. Students should come in their house colours.

title: Library Books Return Deadline
body: All students who have borrowed books from the university library are reminded to return them by May 20, 2026. Late returns will incur a fine. Students with outstanding books will not receive their semester reports.

title: New Teaching Resources Available
body: The university has acquired new teaching resources including updated textbooks for Mathematics and Sciences. These resources are available in the lecturers' lounge. Lecturers are encouraged to review them before the next semester.

title: Holiday Notice: National Heroes Day
body: Please be informed that the university will be closed on February 1, 2026 in observance of National Heroes Day. Normal lectures will resume on February 2, 2026.

title: Scholarship Applications Open
body: Applications for the Greenfield Academy Excellence Scholarship are now open. Students in Year 3 and Year 4 with a GPA above 3.0 are eligible to apply. Application forms are available at the administration office. Deadline: May 31, 2026.

title: Mandatory Staff Meeting
body: All academic staff are required to attend a mandatory meeting on May 20, 2026 at 3:00 PM in the conference room. Agenda: Review of academic calendar, grading policy updates, and preparation for end-of-semester assessments. Attendance is compulsory.
